channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: C&K CEO announces resignation
item.description: C&K Chief Executive Officer, Dr Sandra Cheeseman, has announced she will step down from the role later this year, after seven years at C&K, including five years as CEO.
item.content:encoded: C&K Chief Executive Officer, Dr Sandra Cheeseman, has announced she will step down from the role later this year, after seven years at C&K, including five years as CEO.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Australian Childcare Alliance calls for long-term certainty for wages funding for the early learning sector
item.description: The Australian Childcare Alliance (ACA) is concerned about the lack of certainty regarding wage funding for the early learning workforce in the Federal Government’s 2026–27 Budget. The absence of any funding commitment raises serious concerns about workforce stability across the sector.
item.content:encoded: The Australian Childcare Alliance (ACA) is concerned about the lack of certainty regarding wage funding for the early learning workforce in the Federal Government’s 2026–27 Budget. The absence of any funding commitment raises serious concerns about workforce stability across the sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Government outlines support for G8 Education workers and families following centre suspensions
item.description: The Australian Government has released a dedicated support page for G8 Education Limited workers and their families, following the provider’s decision to suspend a number of early childhood education and care (ECEC) services across Australia.
item.content:encoded: The Australian Government has released a dedicated support page for G8 Education Limited workers and their families, following the provider’s decision to suspend a number of early childhood education and care (ECEC) services across Australia.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Australian Childcare Alliance calls for long-term certainty for wages funding for the early learning sector - The Sector
item.description: Australian Childcare Alliance calls for long-term certainty for wages funding for the early learning sector - The Sector
item.content:encoded: Australian Childcare Alliance calls for long-term certainty for wages funding for the early learning sector - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Providers seek clarity as childcare wage subsidy deadline approaches
item.description: Early childhood education and care providers are calling for certainty as the sector approaches the scheduled end of the ECEC Worker Retention Payment on 30 November 2026.
item.content:encoded: Early childhood education and care providers are calling for certainty as the sector approaches the scheduled end of the ECEC Worker Retention Payment on 30 November 2026.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Honour and Plaîs Early Learning welcomes Zalee Turner as new CEO
item.description: Honour and Plaîs Early Learning has announced the appointment of Zalee Turner as its new Chief Executive Officer, marking an important milestone in the organisation’s continued growth.
item.content:encoded: Honour and Plaîs Early Learning has announced the appointment of Zalee Turner as its new Chief Executive Officer, marking an important milestone in the organisation’s continued growth.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: ELV Barribina opens doors for educator recruitment open day
item.description: The Fair Work Commission has released new Annual Wage Review 2026 material, with the outcome set to influence educator wages, workforce retention and service viability across early childhood education and care.
item.content:encoded: The Fair Work Commission has released new Annual Wage Review 2026 material, with the outcome set to influence educator wages, workforce retention and service viability across early childhood education and care.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Turning the corner isn’t enough: ECEC now needs system conditions that stick
item.description: For years, early childhood education and care (ECEC) has been defined by pressure: workforce shortages, rising costs, reform fatigue and operational instability.
item.content:encoded: For years, early childhood education and care (ECEC) has been defined by pressure: workforce shortages, rising costs, reform fatigue and operational instability.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: NSW Early Learning Commission launches new website
item.description: The NSW Early Learning Commission has launched a new landing page within the NSW Government website, marking the beginning of a staged transition to a new digital home for early childhood education and care regulatory information in NSW.
item.content:encoded: The NSW Early Learning Commission has launched a new landing page within the NSW Government website, marking the beginning of a staged transition to a new digital home for early childhood education and care regulatory information in NSW.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Building leadership pathways the next generation of ECEC professionals wants to step into - The Sector

item.content:encoded: Building leadership pathways the next generation of ECEC professionals wants to step into - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 15 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Budget Falls Short for Aboriginal Children Despite Community-Led Solutions on the Table
item.description: Budget 2026 is one of missed opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children and families.

item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Sector Peak Urges NT Government to Scrap Legislative Changes and Partner on Child Protection
item.description: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Investigation needed into child protection leaks

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 1 May 2026
item.description: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.content:encoded: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC
